LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JULIA DUKHOVNY'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT; MOTION TO QUASH; MOTION AND NOTICE OF MOTION VACATING AND SETTING ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND GRANTING LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED WITH PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT SUBMITS THE DECLARATION OF [DEFENDANT] JULIA DUKHOVNY AS THE ONLY EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF HER ASSERTION THAT DEFENDANT WAS NEVER PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT. IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF PROVIDES DEPOSITION TESTIMONY FROM DEFENDANT'S HUSBAND STATING THAT DEFENDANT'S "ENGLISH IS NOT EVEN GOOD FOR SESAME STREET." (EX. 3, DEPOSITION OF TOLY DUKHOVNY, P. 29:15.) IN REPLY, DEFENDANT DOES NOT ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S ASSERTION THAT DEFENDANT DOES NOT SPEAK ENGLISH. DEFENDANT ALSO DOES NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT SHE WROTE AND UNDERSTOOD HER DECLARATION. BECAUSE AN OATH FROM A TRANSLATOR WAS NOT FILED PURSUANT TO EVID. CODE SEC. 751(C), THE MOTION IS DENIED WITH PREJUDICE FOR LACK OF CREDIBLE EVIDENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR # 5963

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANATOLY DUKHOVNEY'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. MR. DUKHOVNY CLAIMS THAT THE FUNDS ARE SEPARATE PROPERTY. SEE CCP 703.020(B)(2). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON THIRD PARTY CLAIM OF ANATOLY DUKHOVNY- THE COURT ORDERS THE FUNDS RETURNED TO MR. DUKHOVNY. A HEARING TO RESOLVE THE ISSUE WAS NOT CALENDARED IN A TIMELY FASHION PURSUANT TO CCP 720.310(A). EVEN IF THE COURT HAD THE JURISDICTION TO ENTERTAIN THE THIRD PARTY CLAIM, THE COURT WOULD RULE IN FAVOR OF MR. DUKHOVNY. MR. DUKHOVNY'S EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT THE COUPLE WAS SEPARATED AT THE TIME OF THE BIKE ACCIDENT AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT LEVY AGAINST HIS SEPARATE PROPERTY. SEE FAMILY CODE SEC. 910(B). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JULIA DUKHOVNY'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT; MOTION TO QUASH; MOTION VACATING AND SETTING ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND GRANTING LEAVE TO DEFEND IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 11, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MARCH 13, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JON WEBSTER, TEL. NO. (925) 609-7600 EXT. 2264 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. AVNER D. SOFER, TEL. NO. 492-2834 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ANATOLY DUKHOVNEY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANATOLY DUKHOVNEY'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is denied. Mr. Dukhovny claims that the funds are separate property. See CCP 703.020(b)(2). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Hrg Of Third Party Claim
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JON WEBSTER, TEL. NO. (925) 609-7600 EXT. 2264 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. AVNER D. SOFER, TEL. NO. 492-2834 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ANATOLY DUKHOVNEY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Hearing on Third Party Claim of Anatoly Dukhovny- The court orders the funds returned to Mr. Dukhovny. A hearing to resolve the issue was not calendared in a timely fashion pursuant to CCP 720.310(a). Even if the court had the jurisdiction to entertain the third party claim, the court would rule in favor of Mr. Dukhovny. Mr. Dukhovny's evidence indicates that the couple was separated at the time of the bike accident and plaintiff cannot levy against his separate property. See Family Code Sec. 910(b). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default Judgment; Motion To Quash; Motion And Notice Of Motion Vacating And Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment And Granting Leave To Defend And Memo Of P&A;
Off Calendar Apr-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JON WEBSTER, TEL. NO. (925) 609-7600 EXT. 8797 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. AVNER D. SOFER, TEL. NO. 492-2834 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JULIA DUKHOVNY's Motion For Relief From Default Judgment; Motion To Quash; Motion And Notice Of Motion Vacating And Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment And Granting Leave To Defend is Denied with prejudice. Defendant submits the Declaration of [Defendant] Julia Dukhovny as the only evidence in support of her assertion that Defendant was never properly served with the summons and complaint. In opposition, Plaintiff provides deposition testimony from Defendant's husband stating that Defendant's "English is not even good for Sesame Street." (Ex. 3, Deposition of Toly Dukhovny, p. 29:15.) In reply, Defendant does not address Plaintiff's assertion that Defendant does not speak English. Defendant also does not present any evidence showing that she wrote and understood her declaration. Because an oath from a translator was not filed pursuant to Evid. Code sec. 751(c), the motion is denied with prejudice for lack of credible evidence. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963 =302/ehg
